title: CLI instructions to implement GitHub automation hardening modifications
purpose: Apply the architectural hardening changes needed to make the GitHub automation subsystem deterministic, App-authenticated, field-registry-driven, level-triggered, and single-writer governed.

global_assumptions.ssot_authority: Plan YAML/JSON files remain SSOT. GitHub remains execution surface only.

success_criteria: No workflow hardcodes GitHub Project node IDs, field IDs, option IDs, or iteration IDs.
All GitHub Project writes require a valid github_field_registry.json.
All Project writes use GitHub App installation tokens unless an explicit exception is declared.
Phase field writes occur only through the phase advancement evaluator.
Every execution issue has a StepID and stable entry in step_issue_linkage.json.
Reconciliation classifies and corrects drift using explicit drift classes.
Single-writer ownership exists for automation-only fields.

tasks.name: Lock the architecture decision in SSOT docs
tasks.goal: Make JSON/YAML plan authority explicit and prevent future GitHub-as-SSOT drift.
targets: Second Architecture Delta for GitHub Hardening.md
required_changes: Add or confirm language stating that plan files own decomposition, step contracts, phase structure, gate requirements, and evidence requirements.
Add or confirm language stating that GitHub owns only execution-surface concerns such as assignees, comments, and review discussions.
Add or confirm language stating that bidirectional sync to plan-owned fields is forbidden.
acceptance_checks: Document contains explicit statement: 'GitHub is the execution surface, not the control plane.'
Document contains explicit plan-owned vs github-owned distinction.

tasks.name: Create shared CLI command group for GitHub automation
tasks.goal: Centralize all GitHub automation logic into one reusable CLI rather than per-workflow embedded scripts.
required_changes: Each ProgramSpec must declare inputs, outputs, runner_class, preconditions, failure codes, and evidence outputs.
Each ProgramSpec must be invokable by a shared Python CLI.
The CLI must expose one command per ProgramSpec.
acceptance_checks: No workflow contains core business logic beyond setup, auth bootstrap, CLI invocation, and evidence upload.
Each GitHub workflow can map directly to one CLI command.

tasks.name: Standardize on Python runtime for shared CLI
tasks.goal: Avoid runtime fragmentation and align with existing toolchain.
required_changes: Define Python package for GitHub automation CLI.
Add entrypoint for commands: auth, fields-discover, fields-validate, projection-generate, projection-apply, phase-advance-evaluate, reconcile-drift, evidence-seal.
Use structured JSON output for all CLI responses.
acceptance_checks: CLI can be called non-interactively from GitHub Actions.
Each command returns deterministic JSON output and non-zero exit on failure.

tasks.name: Replace PAT-first workflow assumptions with GitHub App auth
tasks.goal: Make auth portable, org-safe, ephemeral, and not tied to an individual user token.
required_changes: Use actions/create-github-app-token@v2 to issue installation token per run.
Remove PAT as default auth path from all workflows that touch Projects.
Allow PAT only behind explicit declared exception block with owner and expiry.
acceptance_checks: All workflows that touch Projects request GitHub App token first.
No workflow defaults to GITHUB_TOKEN or PAT for Projects writes.

tasks.name: Create field registry bootstrap and validation path
tasks.goal: Remove inline field IDs and make field resolution runtime-generated and self-healing.
required_changes: Create schema for github_field_registry.json.
Create workflow that discovers all project fields and options via GraphQL and writes registry file.
Registry must store project_node_id, field_name->field_id, single-select option_name->option_id, iteration identifiers, generated_at, generator_version, api_version, registry_fingerprint.
acceptance_checks: Registry file is generated and schema-valid.
Downstream workflows fail if registry missing or invalid.
No inline PVTF_, PVTSSF_, or PVTIF_ IDs remain in workflows or scripts.

tasks.name: Add lint rule for hardcoded GitHub field identifiers
tasks.goal: Prevent future reintroduction of inline field IDs.
required_changes: Implement scan for strings matching PVTF_, PVTSSF_, PVTIF_.
Fail workflow if hardcoded IDs are detected outside generated registry artifacts.
acceptance_checks: Lint fails when inline field IDs are added to workflow YAML or Python code.
Generated registry files are exempted if needed.

tasks.name: Define projection manifest schema
tasks.goal: Formalize desired GitHub state produced from the plan package.
required_changes: Define manifest root fields: manifest_version, generated_at, plan_hash, projection_version, steps[].
Define per-step fields: step_id, title, body, phase, milestone_group, gate_requirements, repo, labels, assignees, plan_hash, projection_version.
acceptance_checks: GH_PROJECTION_GENERATE emits schema-valid manifest.
Projection apply consumes only schema-valid manifest.

tasks.name: Define step_issue_linkage schema and stable join behavior
tasks.goal: Create deterministic traceability between plan StepIDs and GitHub issues/project items.
required_changes: Use StepID as top-level key or stable unique field.
Store issue_number, issue_node_id, project_item_id, milestone_title, plan_hash, projection_version, created_at, updated_at.
Guarantee all execution issues have a matching linkage record.
acceptance_checks: All projected issues are represented in linkage file.
Reconcile-drift can detect missing linkage and classify orphan issues.

tasks.name: Make one step contract equal one issue explicit
tasks.goal: Remove ambiguity in work granularity.
required_changes: Add explicit rule: one step contract = one GitHub issue.
Allow sub-issues only for declared sub-steps or bounded decomposition.
Clarify that milestones are delivery checkpoints, not phases.
acceptance_checks: Docs contain explicit granularity rule.
Projection logic maps one step to one issue.

tasks.name: Implement deterministic projection apply behavior
tasks.goal: Create/update issues and project items idempotently from manifest.
required_changes: If step_id absent from linkage, create issue then add to project then set fields.
If step_id present and PlanHash changed, update issue body and automation-only fields.
If desired and observed PlanHash match, skip mutation.
Write updated linkage file after successful mutation.
acceptance_checks: Running projection apply twice yields same result.
Duplicate issue creation does not occur for same StepID.

tasks.name: Enable linkage file commit step
tasks.goal: Remove placeholder behavior that prevents state persistence.
required_changes: Replace any disabled placeholder such as if:false on linkage commit step with real success-based condition.
Commit linkage changes only if file content changed.
acceptance_checks: Linkage file commits after successful apply when modified.
No-op run does not create empty commit.

tasks.name: Create field option spec and allowed enumerations
tasks.goal: Formalize allowed values for automation-managed fields.
required_changes: Define allowed options for EvidenceState, GateStatus, Status, and any other automation-only single-select fields.
Phase options must derive from plan or plan package metadata.
Ensure registry validation confirms required option names exist in GitHub.
acceptance_checks: Phase evaluator and reconciliation logic read option values from spec + registry.
Invalid field values are rejected.

tasks.name: Implement composite phase advancement evaluator
tasks.goal: Ensure phase changes are based on full exit-criteria evaluation, not a single event.
required_changes: Evaluator must read plan package, linkage file, field registry, and observed GitHub state.
Evaluator must check all issues closed, all evidence sealed, all gates passed, and milestone completion if phase requires milestone.
Evaluator must output decision artifact with outcome ADVANCE, HOLD, or BLOCKED.
Only evaluator may write Phase field.
acceptance_checks: No non-evaluator workflow writes Phase field.
Evaluator can run on schedule and on key events.
Decision artifact is persisted for every run.

tasks.name: Create explicit field ownership matrix
tasks.goal: Prevent state collisions between humans, workflows, and CLI.
required_changes: Define plan-owned fields.
Define github-owned fields.
Define automation-only fields.
Assign exactly one writer to each automation-only field.
acceptance_checks: Governance file contains single-writer ownership mapping.
Reconciliation uses ownership matrix when classifying drift.

tasks.name: Add unauthorized UI edit drift class
tasks.goal: Catch and correct manual edits to automation-owned fields.
required_changes: Add drift classes: CLEAN, STALE, VIOLATION, ORPHAN, MISSING, OVERRIDE, UNAUTHORIZED_UI_EDIT.
UNAUTHORIZED_UI_EDIT must apply when automation-only field changed outside named writer path and no override exists.
Reconciliation must revert when safe and emit evidence comment/report.
acceptance_checks: Manual edits to automation-only fields are flagged and reverted unless override exists.

tasks.name: Implement override registry
tasks.goal: Allow approved divergence without forcing operators to bypass governance.
required_changes: Store override subject, scope, reason, approver, expiry, and suppressed drift classes.
Reconciliation must read override registry before applying corrections.
acceptance_checks: Named override suppresses correction only for declared scope and time window.
Expired override no longer suppresses correction.

tasks.name: Create level-triggered reconciliation workflow
tasks.goal: Make GitHub state self-healing rather than event-fragile.
required_changes: Run on schedule, push, and manual dispatch.
Fetch all project items and relevant fields.
Compare observed state against linkage file, projection registry, field ownership policy, and override registry.
Classify drift and perform safe convergent corrections.
Never delete issues automatically.
Emit reconciliation report every run.
acceptance_checks: Missed webhook does not permanently break state.
Reconciliation report is produced on every run.
Recoverable drift is corrected automatically.

tasks.name: Add evidence sealing linkage
tasks.goal: Tie passed gates and evidence bundles back to issue/project state.
required_changes: After all required gate checks pass, post evidence comment to linked issue.
Set EvidenceState = Sealed.
Set GateStatus = Passed.
Write machine-readable evidence output.
acceptance_checks: Issue comment includes run_id, bundle_path, and hash.
EvidenceState and GateStatus change only after required checks pass.

tasks.name: Enforce no issue without StepID and no PR without linked issue
tasks.goal: Prevent bypass of plan traceability and gate enforcement.
required_changes: Validate PR body contains Closes #N or Fixes #N.
Validate referenced issue exists in linkage file.
Validate execution issues carry StepID field.
Block merge or flag orphan issue on violation.
acceptance_checks: PR without linked issue fails status check.
Execution issue without StepID becomes ORPHAN drift.

tasks.name: Add runner platform policy
tasks.goal: Prevent environment drift across Linux and Windows assumptions.
required_changes: Define category-to-runner-class mapping.
Require each ProgramSpec to declare runner_class.
Fail validation if ProgramSpec omits runner_class.
acceptance_checks: All ProgramSpecs declare runner_class.
Workflows align with runner policy.

tasks.name: Add rate-limit budget and retry policy
tasks.goal: Make autonomous reconciliation stable under GraphQL/API limits.
required_changes: Define RATE_LIMIT_BUDGET, BACKOFF_POLICY, retry jitter, and low-budget behavior.
Require projection apply and reconciliation commands to honor this policy.
acceptance_checks: CLI backs off on 429 and low remaining budget.
No blind rapid retry loops remain.

tasks.name: Thin existing workflows into wrappers
tasks.goal: Move business logic out of workflow YAML into CLI commands.
required_changes: Limit workflow steps to checkout, auth token issuance, Python setup, CLI call, artifact upload, and optional commit.
Remove embedded business decisions where duplicated.
acceptance_checks: Core logic lives in CLI and ProgramSpecs, not YAML.
Workflow YAML size and logic branching are reduced.

tasks.name: Create validation suite for deterministic spine
tasks.goal: Prove the hardening changes are wired and enforceable.
required_changes: Test missing registry fail-closed behavior.
Test repeated projection apply idempotency.
Test only phase evaluator can write Phase.
Test manual automation-only field edit becomes drift.
Test orphan issue detection.
Test hardcoded ID lint failure.
acceptance_checks: All critical deterministic tests pass.

critical_rules_to_encode.statement: Plan files are SSOT. GitHub is projection/execution surface only.
GitHub Projects writes require GitHub App auth by default.
No inline field IDs or option IDs are permitted.
One step contract equals one GitHub issue.
No phase transition without composite evaluator decision artifact.
Automation-only fields have exactly one writer.

defer_until_after_core_spine: Backlog hygiene refinement rules beyond baseline scans
